title: neonauclea hagenii subsp. hagenii
intro: Neonauclea hagenii subsp. hagenii, also known as the "Benguet medicine tree" or "wild rose", is a plant species native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Neonauclea hagenii subsp. hagenii include its ability to reduce inflammation, prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
